opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was brushing my teeth and saw these white webs all over the inside of my cheeks. I googled it and I'm having a panic attack because it looks exactly like mouth cancer.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I noticed them two hours ago and now my throat feels like it's closing — I'm terrified.

symptom_narrative.onset: noticed 2 hours ago (lesions likely present for months)
symptom_narrative.character: painless white lacy lines inside the cheeks; subjective throat tightness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: since I noticed and googled it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: looking in my mouth with a torch, reading about cancer
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: palpitations, fast breathing; can speak and swallow water; no lip/tongue swelling, no noisy breathing

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: compulsively checking mouth with a mirror and torch

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told it isn't mouth cancer

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, fearful, cites what she read

objective_data_offered.value: I can talk in full sentences and swallow water; my breathing isn't noisy; the white lines are lacy and on both cheeks
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report over video; reassuring for airway/benign morphology but not a formal oral examination
